summary: Expands the apply-mode pilot scope guard to include ci_wait and post_merge_memory, completing the full autonomous issue-to-merge loop. ci_wait polls gh pr checks until required checks pass, fail, or time out. post_merge_memory creates a closure branch, marks the scope completed, writes closure.json, commits, pushes, opens a PR with auto-merge. Apply-mode now always writes the run artifact to disk. Test updated: apply-unsupported-op uses issue_create (ci_wait is now supported). All validators and CLI tests pass.
policy_result: Compliant. Script changes limited to the apply-mode execution block. No secrets, production, billing, auth, database, CI/CD security, or deployment changes.
risk_result: Medium risk (script behavior change). post_merge_memory makes git commits and pushes to new branches. ci_wait can block up to 10 minutes polling CI. Both operations are gated behind apply_allowed and the pilot scope guard.
unresolved_risks: GitHub CI and PR merge pending for issue 140 branch.
ci_wait polls with 30s interval, max 20 attempts (10 min). Long CI runs may time out.
post_merge_memory creates a new branch per task. Stale branches may accumulate if PRs are not cleaned up.
merge_status: PR pending CI. Branch pushed; auto-merge will be enabled after PR creation.
